CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF FEB-04-2015 CONTINUED TO MAR-18-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-23-2015 TO FEB-23-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 23, 2015 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302 (302/AJR)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-23-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE TO SUPPORT THE INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CLAIM IS TIME BARRED. FIRST, THE 3-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPEARS TO APPLY BECAUSE THE CLAIM SOUNDS IN DECEIT. SECOND, EVEN IF THE COURT ACCEPTED DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS, IT IS UNCLEAR WHEN THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS WOULD HAVE BEGUN TO RUN BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT FACIALLY DISCLOSE WHEN PLAINTIFF EXPERIENCED APPRECIABLE HARM. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-23-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. PHILIP L. PILLSBURY, JR., ESQ. and ERIC K. LARSON, ESQ. of Pillsbury & Coleman LLP, Tel No. 415-433-8000 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. KEVIN K. FITZGERALD, ESQ. of Jones, Bell, Abbott, Fleming & Fitzgerald LLP, Tel No. 213-485-1555 appearing for Defendant Amwins Insurance Brokers of California, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMWINS INSURANCE BROKERS OF CALIFORNIA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is denied. Plaintiff has submitted evidence which raises a triable issue whether defendant made an actionable false representation to plaintiff as to a past or existing material fact. At the March 15, 2011 meeting defendant stated to plaintiff that Scottsdale Insurance was the "right" company and "the best company" for plaintiff, that it offered the broadest coverage possible, and that plaintiff's experience with the Osman claim would not be repeated. (Opposing Party's Response and Supporting Evidence # 17.) The fact that the statements were made as the Osman claim was pending, along with Mr. Darville's admission that he would have reviewed the policy prior to the meeting, creates a disputed issue whether the statements were misrepresentations meant to induce plaintiff to renew its policy. Because plaintiff provided evidence which raises a disputed issue whether defendant made a misrepresentation to plaintiff at the March 15, 2011, meeting, plaintiff's negligent misrepresentation claim is for deceit and sounds in fraud and thus the three year statute of limitations applies to the second cause of action. (Broberg v. Guardian Life Ins. Co. of America (2009) 171 Cal.App. 4th 912, 920.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Set For Trial On Nov-09-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Continued To Apr-29-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMWINS INSURANCE BROKERS OF CALIFORNIA, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges justifiable reliance to support the intentional misrepresentation cause of action. Defendant fails to show that the negligent misrepresentation claim is time barred. First, the 3-year statute of limitations appears to apply because the claim sounds in deceit. Second, even if the court accepted defendant's arguments, it is unclear when the statute of limitations would have begun to run because the complaint does not facially disclose when plaintiff experienced appreciable harm. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/EHG)
